In short, the only relief that we have is if this administrative process established by Congress to monitor such exercises of tribal authority is upheld and respected by the Secretary. That in fact is exactly what the Secretary did in connection with the Hopi Indian Tribe, which of course resides on the land situated within the exterior boundaries of the Navajo Tribe.
